page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8369B processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, providing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate mixed performance capabilities across different workloads. The processor achieves average single-core performance in stress-ng (1843.28) and average single-threaded compression speeds, such as 28.01 GB/sec in gzip. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core (3875.1) and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression (21.67 GB/sec) registering in the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large block read and write speeds falling into the lower quartile at 29.48 GB/sec and 24.4 GB/sec respectively.

Given the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for general-purpose workloads. It demonstrates average performance in static web serving (6.15 GB/sec) and small-model LLM inference, processing 403.92 tokens/sec for prompts on a 135M model. However, its lower database operations score (2034.77 in PassMark) and weak Redis SET performance (3.98 million operations/sec) suggest it is less optimal for high-throughput database tasks. The instance is suited for moderate web serving, basic application hosting, and lightweight serialization tasks where single-core performance is prioritized over heavy multi-threaded or memory-intensive operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. It provides 4 dedicated physical cores (4 vCPUs) and 16.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1843.28 and average single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower-tier multi-core stress-ng and uncached memory read/write results. This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, lightweight application hosting, and small-scale LLM inference, though it is less optimal for intensive database operations or highly parallelized computing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8369B CPU. It offers average single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and balanced memory allocation for standard application workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no local storage
Average single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth
Suitable for static web serving, basic application hosting, and small-model inference
